title: jasminum nummulariifolium var. nummulariifolium
intro: Jasminum nummulariifolium var. nummulariifolium, also known as the coin-leaved jasmine, is a plant species in the family Oleaceae, characterized by its coin-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Jasminum nummulariifolium var. nummulariifolium include treating various skin conditions, such as acne, wounds, and eczema, as well as providing relief from fever and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the flowers to create a fragrant oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
